CITY OF NEWPORT BEACH
OFFICE OF THE CITY ATTORNEY
po. Box 176t, NEWPoRT BEACH, CA 92659-1768
(714) 644-3t3t
April 8, l-992
192
Ms. Deanne Stone, Esq.
Staff counsel
California Fair Political
Practices Comnission
428 J Street, Ste. 800
P. O. Box 807
Sacramento, cA
95804-0807
AP:
Re: Request for Formal written Advicei
Letter No. 92 -18 o/councilmember Evelyn Hart
Dear lils. stone:
This letter will- suppl,ement information you have already
received relative to the request of councilmember Evelyn Hart for
fomal written advice. As you know, the request pertains to the
ability of counciLmenber Hart to participate in the process leading
to the adoption of a Specifj-c Area Plan for a portion of her
district 
known as old Newport Boulevard.
In 1.970, Councilmember Hart and her husband purchased property
at 506 Orange. The property is a duplex on an R-2 fot. 
The
property s/as purchased prior to Councilmernber Hart's election in
197A. The units are rarely, if ever, vacant.
Old Newport BouLevard has been designated as a specific plan
area since 1973. On February 24, 1992, the city Council voted to
initiate a general plan amendment to designate a portion of the OId
Newport Boulevard Specific Plan Area for rrrnixed userr (comrnercia] on
first 
floor and residentj-al- on second) . 
The area is now zoned
strictly commercial. The Councj,l also voted to consider expanding
or modifying the boundaries of the Specific Area p]an for OLd
Newport Boulevard. The property owned by Councifmember Hart if in
an area that some resi,dents want rezoned to a ]ower floor area
ratio (from 2.0 to L.5). The property owned by councilmember Hart
is rnore than 3OO feet from the area designated for possible
amendment and the value of her property would be unaffected by any
such amendment. The Council- also asked staff to prepare a
resolution establishing a Specific Area plan Ad Hoc Comnittee and
a scope of services for an economic base study.
3300 Newport Boulevard, Newport Beach


Deanne stone, Esq.
Fair Political Practices Commission
April 8, 1992
Page 2.
On lilarch 23, 1992, the City Council- approved, with
Councilmember Hart abstaining, a resol-utj-on establishing an Ad Hoc
Committee with the responsibility to rtprovide input and guidance
for preparation and adoption of the old Ne$/port Boulevard Specific
Area Plan.rr The Mayor postponed appointments to the committee
pending your response to this request for advice. ordlnarily, the
Mayor hrould appoint Eve1yn Hart as the chairperson of the Ad Hoc
committee since she is the Councilnember representing the district
in which the property is J,ocated.
The Ad Eoc Comnittee would typically hold a series of meetings
with business or^rners, property o$/ners, affected resj-dents and any
other interested parties to receive ideas as to appropriate land
use, development standards and public improvements for the area.
These rneetings are generaLly open to the public with appropriate
notice given. 
over time, the committee would try to develop a
consensus on plan components and, with staff assistance, develop a
draft specific area plan. The draft plan would then be subnitted
to the Planning Commission and City Council as a reconlnendation of
the committee. fn the past, the PLanning Commission and city
council have significantly modified draft plans subnitted by Ad Hoc
Conmittees.
The City Councj-l has approved a number of specific area p1ans,
These plans contain a number of components including permitted land
uses, specific developrnent standards, preferred architectural
styles and thenes, and proposed public improvements. The public
improvement components for other specific area pl,ans have included
the improvement of streets and sidewalks, lnstallation 
of
landscaping, installation of special theme street lightj.ng, and
construction of public facilities 
such as restrooms. The public
improvernent component of the oLd Newport Boulevard Specj-fic Area
Plan would probably not include construction of visitors serving
public facilities 
since the area is not frequented by tourists or
beach-goers .
Councilmember l{artrs property j-s within 85 feet of the
boundaries of the Specific Area P1an. 
The property is
approximately 100 feet from the closest parcel- within the Speaific
Area PIan boundary. The property is located adjacent to the north
eld of the Specific Area Ptan boundary and none of the parcels
within the specific Plan Area front on orange Avenue. The nine
parcels within the Specific Area PLan boundary that are closest to
the Hart property face OId Newport Boulevard. The vast majority of
the parcels within the Specific PIan Area are more than 3OO feet
fron the Hart residence. It is extrenely unlikely that the public
facility 
component of the OLd Newport Boulevard Specific Area plan


Deanne Stone, Esq.
Fair PoliticaL Practices commission
April 8, 1,9 92
Page 3.
would result in any significant improvement to orange Avenue or
clay Street (see map attached). This office has no infornation
that wouLd suggest adoption of a Specific Area Pl-an for O1d Newport
Boulevard would have any affect on the value of the Hart property.
councj.lmember Hart would like you to address the following
questions:
1. 
Does her ownership of the property at 506 orange Avenue
preclude her from accepting an appointnent to the city Council
Ad Hoc Specific Area PLan Comrnittee:
(a) assuming the boundaries of the Specific Area Pfan will
not be modified to include the Hart property;
(b) assuming the boundaries of the Specific Area Plan hrill be
rnodif ied to include the Hart property;
2. 
Assuming councilmember Hart is precluded from mernbership on
the Specific Area PIan Committee, are there any constraints on
Councilmernber Hartrs ability to:
(a) attend meetings as a member of the general publ-ic;
(b) provide input during Cornmittee rneetingsi
(c) or, provide written conments to the Comrnittee.
In the absence of any indication as to the contents of the
Specific Area PIan, are there any criteria 
useful to
Councilmember Hart in deterrnining whether a Specific PIan
would have any impact on the value of her property?
RHB:kmc
Attachnent
fppcha.l.tr
3.
H. Burnham
Attorney
